heading: Other assessable penalties with respect to the preparation of tax returns for other persons
label: 6695 Other assessable penalties with respect to the preparation of tax returns for other persons
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

Amendments2018—Subsec. (h)(1). , inserted “an amount equal to” after “increased by” and “for the calendar year” after “section 1(f)(3)”.Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(a)(299)(C)Subsec. (h)(2). , substituted “paragraph (1)” for “subparagraph (A)” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–141, § 401(a)(309)Subsec. (h)(2)(B). , substituted “subparagraph (A)” for “clause (i)”.Pub. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any person who is a tax return preparer with respect to any return or claim for refund who fails to comply with due diligence requirements imposed by the Secretary by regulations with respect to determining eligibility for, or the amount of, the credit allowable by section 24, 25A(a)(1), or 32 shall pay a penalty of $500 for each such failure.”Pub. children.heading: Failure to furnish copy to taxpayer
children.label: a Failure to furnish copy to taxpayer
children.text: Any person who is a tax return preparer with respect to any return or claim for refund who fails to comply with section 6107(a) with respect to such return or claim shall pay a penalty of $50 for such failure, unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause and not due to willful neglect. The maximum penalty imposed under this subsection on any person with respect to documents filed during any calendar year shall not exceed $25,000.

children.heading: Failure to sign return
children.label: b Failure to sign return
children.text: Any person who is a tax return preparer with respect to any return or claim for refund, who is required by regulations prescribed by the Secretary to sign such return or claim, and who fails to comply with such regulations with respect to such return or claim shall pay a penalty of $50 for such failure, unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause and not due to willful neglect. The maximum penalty imposed under this subsection on any person with respect to documents filed during any calendar year shall not exceed $25,000.

children.heading: Failure to furnish identifying number
children.label: c Failure to furnish identifying number
children.text: Any person who is a tax return preparer with respect to any return or claim for refund and who fails to comply with section 6109(a)(4) with respect to such return or claim shall pay a penalty of $50 for such failure, unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause and not due to willful neglect. The maximum penalty imposed under this subsection on any person with respect to documents filed during any calendar year shall not exceed $25,000.

children.heading: Failure to retain copy or list
children.label: d Failure to retain copy or list
children.text: Any person who is a tax return preparer with respect to any return or claim for refund who fails to comply with section 6107(b) with respect to such return or claim shall pay a penalty of $50 for each such failure, unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause and not due to willful neglect. The maximum penalty imposed under this subsection on any person with respect to any return period shall not exceed $25,000.

children.heading: Failure to file correct information returns
children.label: e Failure to file correct information returns

children.text: each failure to file a return as required under such section, and
each failure to set forth an item in the return as required under section,

children.heading: Negotiation of check
children.label: f Negotiation of check
children.text: Any person who is a tax return preparer who endorses or otherwise negotiates (directly or through an agent) any check made in respect of the taxes imposed by this title which is issued to a taxpayer (other than the tax return preparer) shall pay a penalty of $500 with respect to each such check. The preceding sentence shall not apply with respect to the deposit by a bank (within the meaning of section 581) of the full amount of the check in the taxpayer’s account in such bank for the benefit of the taxpayer.

children.heading: Failure to be diligent in determining eligibility for certain tax benefits
children.label: g Failure to be diligent in determining eligibility for certain tax benefits

children.text: eligibility to file as a head of household (as defined in section 2(b)) on the return, or
eligibility for, or the amount of, the credit allowable by section 24, 25A(a)(1), or 32,

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any failure relating to a return or claim for refund filed in a calendar year beginning after 2014, each of the dollar amounts under subsections (a), (b), (c), (d), (e), (f), and (g) shall be increased by an amount equal to such dollar amount multiplied by the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year determined by substituting “calendar year 2013” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: is not less than $5,000 and is not a multiple of $500, such amount shall be rounded to the next lowest multiple of $500, and

children.label: B
children.text: is not described in subparagraph (A) and is not a multiple of $5, such amount shall be rounded to the next lowest multiple of $5.
